 {"id":"bd-17kf","title":"Implement Time-Travel Debugging tests","description":"# Implement Time-Travel Debugging tests

## Background
Part of Time-Travel Debugging (bd-2lwf). Tests ensure frame snapshots work correctly.

## Unit Tests
- snapshot_captures_full_frame_state: Buffer, cursor, style all captured
- delta_encoding_produces_smaller_output: Delta < full snapshot for similar frames
- decompression_matches_original: Decompress(compress(frame)) == frame
- navigation_moves_through_history: previous()/next() work correctly
- seek_to_index_works: seek(n) jumps to correct frame
- ring_buffer_evicts_oldest: Old frames evicted when capacity reached

## Property Tests
- delta_roundtrip_is_lossless: Any frame survives delta encode/decode cycle
- frame_ordering_is_preserved: Frames come out in insertion order

## Integration Tests
- full_session_recording_and_replay: Record session, scrub through history
- export_to_file_and_reimport: Save history to disk, load it back

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples
- [ ] Memory usage bounded by ring buffer size
- [ ] Frame navigation is smooth (< 1ms per seek)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:24.133973381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:16:08.661931554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:16:08.661911236Z","close_reason":"TimeTravel tests already implemented in crates/ftui-harness/src/time_travel.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17kf","depends_on_id":"bd-2lwf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-17nn","title":"Widget Introspection Debug Overlay","description":"# Widget Introspection Debug Overlay

## Background & Motivation

When developing complex widget layouts, developers need to see:
- Widget boundaries
- Widget names and types
- Render times per widget
- Hit test areas for mouse events

**The solution:**
- Debug overlay toggleable at runtime (F12 or config)
- Zero overhead when disabled
- Rich visual feedback during development

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.7 (Widgets Ring) for widget tree access
- Complements bd-9xbn (Layout Constraint Debugger)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct DebugOverlay {
    enabled: bool,
    show_boundaries: bool,
    show_names: bool,
    show_render_times: bool,
    show_hit_areas: bool,
}

impl DebugOverlay {
    pub fn wrap<W: Widget>(widget: W, name: &str) -> DebugWrapped<W> { ... }
    
    pub fn render_overlay(&self, frame: &Frame, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Draw colored rectangles around each widget
        // Show widget names as labels
        // Show render time in corner
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Overlay toggle (INFO level)
tracing::info!(enabled = enabled, "Debug overlay toggled");

// Widget render time captured (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(
    widget = name,
    render_time_us = render_time.as_micros(),
    area = ?area,
    "Widget render complete"
);
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Enable overlay and take screenshot
./ftui-harness --debug-overlay --screenshot overlay.png

# Verify overlay labels visible
./scripts/verify-overlay.sh overlay.png --expect-widgets "Panel,Button,Input"

# Performance test with overlay
./ftui-harness --debug-overlay --benchmark --assert-overhead-percent 5
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Widget boundaries visible with distinct colors
- [ ] Widget names displayed as labels
- [ ] Render times shown in microseconds
- [ ] Toggle without code changes (F12 or env var)
- [ ] Zero overhead when disabled (compile-time check)
- [ ] Hit areas highlighted on hover
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"Implemented debug overlay widget + stateful wrappers with env-var default enable, F12 toggle helper, hover hit-area highlight, and tracing logs for toggle/render time. Added debug-overlay feature gate + exports. Added unit tests in module. -{"id":"bd-17w24","title":"Mermaid Styling Support (classDef/style/linkStyle)","description":"Background
- Mermaid diagrams use styling directives to convey semantics.
- Terminal constraints mean we map styles to fg/bg/border/accent + glyph choices.

Scope
- Parse classDef/class/style/linkStyle directives from AST/Init.
- Resolve style precedence (node-specific > class > global defaults).
- Apply init themeVariables where supported (mapped to theme tokens).
- Map styles to theme tokens and rendering primitives (fg/bg, border, line style, arrowhead style).
- Clamp to accessible contrast where possible (prevent low-contrast combos).
- Provide graceful fallback for unsupported styles with warnings (compat matrix).

Considerations
- Deterministic mapping; same styles → same tokens.
- Avoid invalid color combos; clamp to theme palette.
- Log style resolution and fallback decisions in evidence logs.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for precedence resolution + style mapping.
- Snapshot tests for styled nodes/edges across Unicode/ASCII modes.

Success Criteria
- Styles applied where supported; unsupported styles degrade predictably.","notes":"Still blocked by mermaid.rs + mermaid-config.md reservations.  {"id":"bd-19cll","title":"Layout Aesthetic Metrics + Tuning Harness","description":"Background
- Layout beauty is a primary goal but needs objective, tunable metrics.

Scope
- Define aesthetic metrics: crossings, bends, edge length variance, symmetry, alignment, compactness.
- Add readability penalties for label collisions and long detours.
- Build a tuning harness to compare layouts with different weights.
- Expose metric scores in evidence logs and debug overlay.
- Provide deterministic weight presets for compact/normal/rich tiers.

Considerations
- Must remain deterministic; no stochastic search.
- Metrics should be cheap enough for interactive use.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for metric computations on known layouts.
- Snapshot tests to ensure preset changes do not regress visuals.

Success Criteria
- Metrics available for decision-making and debugging.
- Presets produce visibly improved layouts at larger sizes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:08.216619699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:24:16.268506554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:24:16.268443105Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19cll","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:13.452960628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19cll","depends_on_id":"bd-2hxl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:19.088240976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3emq","title":"Async Validation: Deterministic Concurrency Contract + Golden Trace","description":"## Goal
Make async validation behavior deterministic under a seeded scheduler and prove no stale results can override newer input.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization + Correctness)
- Monotonic validation token per input change; results must include token.
- Apply result only if token matches current input token.
- Record validation events in a trace (start/cancel/complete/apply).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stale results are always ignored with explicit log entries.
- Deterministic trace under fixed seed and scripted input.
- Golden trace checksum matches after refactors (isomorphism proof).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_token_monotonic: tokens strictly increase per input.
- unit_stale_result_ignored: older token never applies.
- unit_trace_contains_all_events: no missing transitions.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Seeded async validator test harness with golden trace hash.
- Log: token, state, duration, decision per validation.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-3fu8","title":"Semantic Events: Core Types and SemanticEvent Enum","description":"## Task

Define the core `SemanticEvent` enum and related types in `ftui-core`.

## Implementation

Create `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs`:

```rust
use crate::{KeyCode, Modifiers, MouseButton, Position};
use std::time::Duration;

/// High-level semantic events derived from raw terminal input.
/// These represent user intentions rather than raw input.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum SemanticEvent {
    // === Mouse Gestures ===
    
    /// Single click (mouse down + up in same position)
    Click {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Two clicks within threshold time
    DoubleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Three clicks within threshold (often used for line selection)
    TripleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Mouse held down beyond threshold without moving
    LongPress {
        pos: Position,
        duration: Duration,
    },
    
    // === Drag Gestures ===
    
    /// Mouse moved beyond drag threshold while button held
    DragStart {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Ongoing drag movement
    DragMove {
        start: Position,
        current: Position,
        delta: (i16, i16),  // Movement since last DragMove
    },
    
    /// Mouse released after drag
    DragEnd {
        start: Position,
        end: Position,
    },
    
    /// Drag cancelled (Escape pressed, focus lost, etc.)
    DragCancel,
    
    // === Keyboard Gestures ===
    
    /// Key chord sequence completed (e.g., Ctrl+K Ctrl+C)
    Chord {
        sequence: Vec<ChordKey>,
    },
    
    // === Touch-Like Gestures ===
    
    /// Swipe gesture (rapid mouse movement)
    Swipe {
        direction: SwipeDirection,
        distance: u16,
        velocity: f32,  // cells per second
    },
}

/// A key in a chord sequence
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct ChordKey {
    pub code: KeyCode,
    pub modifiers: Modifiers,
}

/// Direction for swipe gestures
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum SwipeDirection {
    Up,
    Down,
    Left,
    Right,
}

impl SemanticEvent {
    /// Returns true if this is a drag-related event
    pub fn is_drag(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self, Self::DragStart { .. } | Self::DragMove { .. } | Self::DragEnd { .. } | Self::DragCancel)
    }
    
    /// Returns the position if this event has one
    pub fn position(&self) -> Option<Position> {
        match self {
            Self::Click { pos, .. } |
            Self::DoubleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::TripleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::LongPress { pos, .. } |
            Self::DragStart { pos, .. } => Some(*pos),
            Self::DragMove { current, .. } => Some(*current),
            Self::DragEnd { end, .. } => Some(*end),
            _ => None,
        }
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_is_drag() {
        assert\!(SemanticEvent::DragStart { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
        
        assert\!(\!SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
    }

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_position() {
        let click = SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 5, y: 10 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        };
        assert_eq\!(click.position(), Some(Position { x: 5, y: 10 }));
        
        let chord = SemanticEvent::Chord { sequence: vec\![] };
        assert_eq\!(chord.position(), None);
    }

    #[test]
    fn chord_key_equality() {
        let k1 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k2 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k3 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('c'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        
        assert_eq\!(k1, k2);
        assert_ne\!(k1, k3);
    }
}
```

- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-3hsz","title":"Text Effects: Shadow and Multi-Layer Rendering","description":"## Background
Flat text lacks depth. Shadows and multi-layer rendering add dimension, improve readability on complex backgrounds, and create professional polish.

## Implementation

Add Shadow struct:
```rust
pub struct Shadow {
    pub dx: i8,
    pub dy: i8,
    pub color: PackedRgba,
    pub opacity: f64,
}
```

Add GlowConfig for multi-layer glow:
```rust
pub struct GlowConfig {
    pub color: PackedRgba,
    pub intensity: f64,    // 0.0-1.0
    pub layers: u8,        // Number of glow layers (1-5)
    pub falloff: f64,      // How quickly glow fades (0.5 = 50% per layer)
}
```

Add to StyledText:
- .shadow(Shadow) - single drop shadow
- .shadows(Vec<Shadow>) - multiple shadow layers
- .glow(GlowConfig) - multi-layer soft glow

NOTE: Outline/stroke is in separate bd-882b bead to keep concerns separate.

Rendering approach:
1. Render shadow/glow layers first (furthest from text first)
2. Each layer offset by (dx*layer, dy*layer) with decreasing opacity
3. Render main text on top
4. Handle edge cases: shadows near terminal bounds use saturating arithmetic

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_shadow_offset_applied: Shadow appears at dx,dy from text
- test_shadow_opacity: Shadow color correctly alpha-blended
- test_shadow_multiple_layers: Vec<Shadow> renders all layers
- test_glow_layer_count: Correct number of glow layers rendered
- test_glow_falloff: Each layer dimmer by falloff factor
- test_shadow_at_edge: No panic at terminal boundaries
- test_shadow_z_order: Main text renders on top of all shadows
- test_glow_intensity_scales: Higher intensity = brighter glow

## Visual Tests
- Render 'SHADOW' with drop shadow (1,1), verify offset
- Render 'GLOW' with 3-layer glow, verify falloff pattern
- Render at terminal edge, verify no overflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Shadow and Multi-Layer Rendering
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3hy","title":"Define per-module unit test coverage requirements","description":"# Testing: Unit Test Coverage Matrix (per crate/module)

## Purpose
This bead defines **explicit, per-crate unit test expectations** so we can enforce correctness while the codebase grows.

This is intentionally opinionated: the kernel is the product, so kernel crates have higher coverage requirements.

## How To Use
- When adding a new module, add it to the matrix.
- When adding a new public API, add explicit unit tests here.
- CI should enforce these thresholds (see bd-xn2).

---

## Coverage Targets (v1)
These are *minimum* targets. For kernel-critical modules, we should aim higher.

- ftui-render: ≥ 85% (kernel)
- ftui-core: ≥ 80% (terminal/session + input)
- ftui-style: ≥ 80%
- ftui-text: ≥ 80%
- ftui-layout: ≥ 75%
- ftui-runtime: ≥ 75%
- ftui-widgets: ≥ 70%
- ftui-extras: ≥ 60% (feature-gated; correctness still required, but scope is optional)

Note: Some integration-heavy paths (PTY tests) will not show up as unit coverage; they are enforced separately.

---

## ftui-render (≥ 85%)
Kernel correctness lives here.

### Cell / CellContent / CellAttrs
- [ ] CellContent creation from char vs grapheme-id
- [ ] Width semantics (ASCII, wide, combining, emoji)
- [ ] Continuation-cell sentinel semantics for wide glyphs
- [ ] PackedRgba: construction + Porter-Duff alpha blending
- [ ] CellAttrs: bitflags operations + merge/override
- [ ] 16-byte Cell layout invariants (size/alignment) + bits_eq correctness

### Buffer
- [ ] Create/resize buffer with dimensions
- [ ] get/set bounds checking + deterministic defaults
- [ ] Clear semantics (full vs region)
- [ ] Scissor stack push/pop semantics (intersection monotonicity)
- [ ] Opacity stack push/pop semantics (product in [0,1])
- [ ] Wide glyph placement + continuation cells
- [ ] Iteration order and row-major storage assumptions

### Diff
- [ ] empty diff (no changes)
- [ ] single cell change
- [ ] row changes
- [ ] run grouping behavior
- [ ] scratch buffer reuse (no unbounded allocations)

### Presenter
- [ ] cursor tracking correctness
- [ ] style tracking correctness
- [ ] link tracking correctness (OSC 8 open/close)
- [ ] single-write-per-frame behavior
- [ ] synchronized output behavior where supported (fallback correctness)

---

## ftui-core (≥ 80%)

### Event types
- [ ] canonical key/mouse/resize/paste/focus event types are stable

### InputParser
- [ ] bounded CSI/OSC/DCS parsing (DoS limits)
- [ ] bracketed paste decoding + max size
- [ ] mouse SGR decoding
- [ ] focus/resize event decoding

### TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] env heuristic detection (TERM/COLORTERM)
- [ ] mux flags (tmux/screen/zellij) correctness

### TerminalSession lifecycle
- [ ] RAII enter/exit discipline
- [ ] panic cleanup paths are idempotent

---

## ftui-style (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Style defaults + builder ergonomics
- [ ] deterministic style merge (explicit masks)
- [ ] Color downgrade (truecolor→256→16→mono)
- [ ] Theme presets + semantic slots
- [ ] StyleSheet registry + named style composition

---

## ftui-text (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Segment system correctness (Cow<str>)
- [ ] width measurement correctness + LRU cache behavior
- [ ] grapheme segmentation helpers for wrap/truncate correctness
- [ ] wrap/truncate semantics for ZWJ/emoji/combining
- [ ] markup parser correctness (feature-gated)

---

## ftui-layout (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Rect operations (intersection/contains)
- [ ] Flex constraint solving + gaps
- [ ] Grid placement + spanning + named areas
- [ ] Measurement algebra (min/max + clamp)

---

## ftui-runtime (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Program/Model/Cmd lifecycle + scheduler
- [ ] determinism invariants (simulator)
- [ ] subscriptions/ticks integration

---

## ftui-widgets (≥ 70%)
Harness essentials must be tested.

- [ ] Panel/Block borders + title placement
- [ ] Paragraph wrapping + alignment
- [ ] Table sizing + selection
- [ ] Viewport/log viewer scrolling
- [ ] TextInput cursor + edit behavior
- [ ] Progress/spinner rendering

---

## Test Quality Requirements
- Each test is deterministic (no wall-clock timing).
- Each test is fast (target <10ms).
- Failures must be actionable (expected vs actual grid/segments, not “assertion failed”).

## Property Testing
Use property tests for:
- diff correctness
- presenter roundtrip via terminal model
- text width/wrap invariants
- layout solver invariants

- [ ] The matrix matches the actual crate boundaries (no stale mapping).
- [ ] CI encodes these thresholds (or a deliberate waiver is documented).

 {"id":"bd-xn2","title":"Create CI/CD pipeline configuration","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - CI/CD PIPELINE

## Background
Continuous integration ensures code quality, test coverage, and performance budgets are maintained. Must run on every PR and push to main.

## Technical Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)

#### Jobs Overview
| Job | Trigger | Purpose | Blocking |
|-----|---------|---------|----------|
| check | PR, push | Format, clippy, tests | Yes |
| coverage | PR, push | Coverage thresholds | Yes |
| benchmarks | push to main | Performance budgets | Warn |
| e2e | PR, push | End-to-end tests | Yes |
| docs | PR, push | Doc build verification | Yes |

#### Check Job
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test --all-features
- cargo test --no-default-features

#### Coverage Job
- Use cargo-llvm-cov for coverage
- Thresholds:
  - Overall: ≥ 70%
  - ftui-core: ≥ 80%
  - ftui-render: ≥ 75%
- Upload to codecov.io

#### Benchmarks Job
- Run on push to main only
- Performance budgets from plan:
  - Cell comparison: < 1ns
  - Present (80×24, 5% changed): p50 < 1.0ms
  - Diff scan: < 100µs
  - Layout solve (20 items): < 100µs
- Fail on 2x regression

#### E2E Job
- Run scripts/e2e_test.sh
- Upload artifacts on failure
- Test on ubuntu-latest and macos-latest

#### Docs Job
- cargo doc --all-features --no-deps
- Verify no broken links
- Upload docs artifact

### Matrix Testing
- OS: ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- Rust: stable, nightly
- Features: default, all, minimal

### Caching
- Cache ~/.cargo/registry
- Cache ~/.cargo/git
- Cache target/ with hash key

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] check job with format/clippy/tests
- [ ] coverage job with thresholds
- [ ] benchmarks job with budgets
- [ ] e2e job with script execution
- [ ] docs job with verification
- [ ] Matrix: 3 OS × 2 toolchains
- [ ] Proper caching configuration
- [ ] Badge in README

 {"id":"bd-y6fw","title":"Keybinding Hints: Utility-Based Ranking (Bayes + VOI)","description":"## Goal
Prioritize which shortcuts to show so users see the most valuable hints first without clutter.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Treat each hint as an action with uncertain utility U and cost C (screen space + attention). Maintain a Bayesian posterior over utility from observed usage and context, then rank by expected value of information (VOI):
- Posterior mean: E[U_i | data]
- Expected net value: V_i = E[U_i] - lambda * C_i
- Optional VOI: prioritize hints whose exposure is expected to reduce uncertainty the most for new users.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ranking is stable under small usage changes (no flicker).
- Deterministic under fixed seed and usage log.
- Supports a "debug" view showing evidence ledger and scores.

## Implementation Notes
- Use Beta-Bernoulli or Normal-Normal depending on usage signal.
- Context features: focused widget, recent command history, mode (insert/normal), and user profile.
- Provide fallback: static ordering if usage data absent.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_prior_updates: posterior updates with usage events.
- unit_ranking_stability: small deltas do not reorder beyond hysteresis.
- property_context_switch: ranking changes only when context changes.
- e2e_usage_replay: deterministic ranking across runs; log evidence ledger.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- PTY script: simulate usage events and verify top-N hints and scores.
- Log: posterior parameters, E[U], C, VOI, final rank.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-yzu","title":"Implement Flex layout with constraint solving","description":"PHASE 5 - FLEX LAYOUT

## Background
Flex layout provides 1D constraint-based sizing for rows/columns. Core layout primitive that most complex layouts build upon. Based on CSS Flexbox concepts adapted for terminal grid.

## Technical Requirements
- Flex struct with direction (Row/Column)
- Constraint types:
  - Fixed(u16): exact size
  - Percent(f32): percentage of parent
  - Min(u16): minimum size, grow to fill
  - Max(u16): maximum size, shrink to fit
  - Ratio(u16, u16): proportional sharing
- Constraint solving algorithm:
  1. Allocate Fixed constraints first
  2. Calculate Percent based on remaining space
  3. Distribute remainder to Min/Ratio proportionally
  4. Clamp Max constraints
- Gap/spacing between items
- Cross-axis alignment: Start, Center, End, Stretch

## Design Rationale
Flex is simpler than full 2D grid but handles 90% of TUI layouts. Constraint types mirror Ratatui's proven model. Solving algorithm is deterministic for predictable layouts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flex struct with direction and items
- [ ] Full constraint type enum
- [ ] Deterministic constraint solver
- [ ] Gap/margin support
- [ ] Cross-axis alignment
- [ ] Nested Flex support
- [ ] Property tests for constraint invariants
- [ ] Edge case handling (overflow, zero space)

## Performance Budget
- Layout solve for 20 items: < 100µs
- No allocations for typical layouts (stack buffer)

 {"id":"bd-z8q6","title":"Migrate simple buffer-only widgets to Frame (Category A)","description":"## Scope

Migrate all Category A widgets - those that only write to buffer and don't need
any special Frame features.

## Migration Pattern

For each widget:

1. Change signature:
   ```rust
   // FROM:
   fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
   
   // TO:
   fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
   ```

2. Replace all `buf` references with `frame.buffer`:
   ```rust
   // FROM:
   buf.get_mut(x, y)
   
   // TO:
   frame.buffer.get_mut(x, y)
   ```

3. Or use a local binding for cleaner code:
   ```rust
   fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
       let buf = &mut frame.buffer;
       // ... rest of code unchanged
   }
   ```

## Widgets Expected in This Category

Based on functionality (to be confirmed by audit):
- Block (borders, title)
- Rule (horizontal/vertical lines)
- Scrollbar (decorative)
- Spinner (animation frames)
- Paragraph (text display)
- Progress (progress bars)

## Important

- DO NOT change any widget behavior
- DO NOT add new Frame features unless explicitly needed
- This is a MECHANICAL migration only
- If a widget turns out to need Frame features, move it to appropriate category

+## Style Resolution (Implemented)
+
+Style directives (`classDef`, `class`, `style`, `linkStyle`) are parsed into
+structured properties and resolved deterministically:
+
+**Supported properties**
+- `fill`, `background`, `background-color`
+- `stroke`, `border-color`
+- `stroke-width`, `border-width` (px allowed)
+- `stroke-dasharray`
+- `color`, `font-color`
+- `font-weight`
+
+Unsupported keys are recorded as `mermaid/unsupported/style` warnings with spans.
+
+**Precedence (last wins)**
+1. `themeVariables` defaults (if present)
+2. `classDef` styles (merged in class list order)
+3. Node-specific `style`
+
+`linkStyle default` applies to all edges; `linkStyle <idx>` applies by edge
+index in **source order** (edges preserve statement order to keep indices stable).
+
+**Theme variables mapped**
+- `primaryColor` → `fill`
+- `primaryTextColor` → `color`
+- `primaryBorderColor` → `stroke`
+
+**Contrast clamp**
+If both `fill` and `color` are set, a minimum contrast ratio is enforced
+via `clamp_contrast` (currently 3.0). When clamped, the source list records
+`contrast-clamp`.
